title: malpighia suberosa
intro: Malpighia suberosa, also known as the Prickly Mallow or Jamaican Cherry, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malpighia suberosa include treating fever, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Malpighia suberosa include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
